pretool-bash-safety.sh: IS_SUBAGENT variable moved to top of script (line 20) to be set once, replacing 3 inline re-parses of $INPUT
check_and_consume_allowlist now references script-global IS_SUBAGENT instead of local is_sub
Removed six per-rule check_and_consume_allowlist calls: systemctl, stash push/save/create/store/-u/--all, bare stash, wide-path checkout, wide-path restore, and revert-with-ref-modifier
Removed duplicate 'Global /allow short-circuit RELOCATED' comment block (~8 lines)
Removed IS_SUBAGENT inline re-parse before /do bypass block
Added inline comment explaining global short-circuit is now sole callsite
pretool-git-privilege-guard.py: Removed unused variable 'sid = _get_session_id(data)' from _evaluate_command (the local 'sid' was assigned but never referenced in the function body)
Added early-exit allowlist fast-path in _evaluate_command for non-push commands

findings.reason: Comment at line 130 references 'global /allow short-circuit at line 555' but the actual callsite is now line 618. This line reference was stale before this diff; not introduced by this cycle.
findings.recommendation: Replace 'at line 555' with a position-independent description such as 'at the global /allow short-circuit below, immediately after the /do bypass'. Avoids brittle line-number references that become stale on every edit.

findings.reason: _is_head_ref function (line 393) is defined but never called anywhere in the file. Pre-existing before this diff; not introduced by this change. No functional references exist, so deletion would not violate Safety Rules.
findings.recommendation: Add a comment documenting _is_head_ref as intentional dead code retained for reference, or remove it in a dedicated cleanup cycle.

observations.reason: _is_overnight_active (line 423) is intentionally retained dead code, explicitly documented in the module docstring at line 72: 'The _is_overnight_active() helper is retained as dead code for reference but is no longer consulted by main()'. No action needed.

The new inline comment added at line 613-617 says 'covers all paths unconditionally'. This is slightly overbroad: the short-circuit covers downstream Bash block-rule paths for main-agent /allow grants; it does not cover non-Bash early exit, /do preemption, or subagent bypass denial (which is handled inside check_and_consume_allowlist itself). A more precise wording would be: 'covers all downstream Bash block-rule paths for main-agent /allow grants; subagents remain denied inside check_and_consume_allowlist'. Out of scope for blocking close — inspector-only observation.

diff_assessment: The diff is a clean refactor. IS_SUBAGENT is now computed once at top-of-script instead of three separate inline parses of $INPUT. Redundant per-rule check_and_consume_allowlist calls (six in total) were consolidated into the single global short-circuit already present before the block rules. In pretool-git-privilege-guard.py, an unused local variable assignment was removed from _evaluate_command and an early-exit allowlist fast-path was added for non-push commands. No new files added, no misplaced docs, no temp files, no orphaned scripts introduced. Two pre-existing minor issues (F1 stale line-number comment, F2 undocumented dead function) are noted as advisory.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex flagged 6 issues: F1 pre-existing/out-of-diff-scope (correct, improve recommendation wording); F2 pre-existing/should be OBSERVATION_ONLY; F3 false positive (already says 'no action needed', move out of findings); F4 internally contradictory category vs. reason (remove); bash comment 'covers all paths unconditionally' is overbroad; diff summary miscounted/mislabeled rules.

findings.issue: F1 stale line-number comment is pre-existing, not diff-introduced; recommendation should avoid brittle line references
findings.proposed_fix: Replace 'at line 555' with a position-independent description: 'at the global /allow short-circuit below, immediately after the /do bypass'
findings.rationale: Recommendation wording updated to avoid brittle line number; F1 retained as minor pre-existing advisory

findings.issue: F2 is pre-existing scope drift; should be OBSERVATION_ONLY for a diff-scoped report
findings.proposed_fix: Mark F2 OBSERVATION_ONLY or remove from findings
findings.rationale: F2 retained in findings as advisory (pre_existing: true, introduced_in_diff: false) which satisfies the default-safe rule — it cannot block close per AC-2.6(b)

findings.issue: F3 is a false positive — inspector's own reason says 'no action needed' for intentionally documented dead code
findings.proposed_fix: Remove F3 from findings; recast as OBSERVATION_ONLY
findings.rationale: F3 moved from findings to observations section

findings.issue: F4 is internally contradictory: categorized 'stale_comment' severity 'minor' but reason says the comment is accurate and there is no cleanliness issue
findings.rationale: F4 removed from the report entirely

findings.issue: New bash comment 'covers all paths unconditionally' is overbroad; does not account for subagent denial or non-Bash early exit
findings.proposed_fix: Change to: 'covers all downstream Bash block-rule paths for main-agent /allow grants; subagents remain denied inside check_and_consume_allowlist'
findings.rationale: Inspector reports findings; does not patch files. Logged as observation O2 for the dev cycle to address.

findings.issue: Diff summary miscounted: said '5 git block rules (stash, checkout, restore, revert, systemctl)' — systemctl is not a git rule and the count is wrong
findings.proposed_fix: Replace with: 'Removed six per-rule check_and_consume_allowlist calls: systemctl, stash push/save/create/store/-u/--all, bare stash, wide-path checkout, wide-path restore, and revert-with-ref-modifier'
findings.rationale: Diff summary corrected in the final report

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Applied 4 corrections: removed F3 (false positive, moved to observations), removed F4 (contradictory), improved F1 recommendation wording to avoid brittle line numbers, corrected diff summary miscounting. Logged overbroad bash comment wording as observation O2 (out of scope for inspector to fix).
